name: Happy Harbour Light & Water Show 欢乐港湾灯光水秀
venue: Bao'an Happy Harbour, by the Bay Glory Ferris wheel
desc: Free nightly fountain, music and light show on the waterfront beside the giant Bay Glory Ferris wheel. Open-air, no ticket, a popular evening outing for families.
notes: Daily except Mondays per bendibao. Confirm schedule closer to the date.

name: Bao'an Coastal Culture Park Light Show 滨海文化公园灯光秀
desc: Free nightly building-facade and waterfront light display in Bao'an's CBD. Easy, open-air evening activity for all ages.
notes: Runs every evening (summer hours). No reservation needed.

name: Civic Center Light Show 福田市民中心灯光秀
desc: Large free animated light show across the Civic Center / Lianhuashan skyline. A classic Shenzhen evening spectacle, fully open-air and family-suitable.
notes: Summer schedule is Fri & Sat only, so within the windows it falls on Fri Jul 10 and Fri Jul 17. May need a free reservation.

name: Talent Park Light Show 人才公园灯光秀
desc: Free lakeside light show in a waterfront park near Shenzhen Bay; pleasant walking space plus the show makes an easy family evening.
notes: Fri & Sat in summer, so only Fri Jul 10 and Fri Jul 17 qualify.

name: Splendid China folk performances 锦绣中华民俗村演出
desc: Daily large-scale folk shows: a war-horse stunt spectacle, costume show, float parade and ethnic-village performances. Open day to night, broadly family-friendly.
notes: Regular daily program continues through summer; confirm 2026 show times on the official site.

name: Dayun Tiandi Craft Beer & Food Festival 精酿啤酒节
desc: Open-air food-and-drink festival with 60+ brands, street food, music and play areas at an outdoor mall plaza. Workable for families with kids.
notes: Overlaps the Jul 6-10 window. Primarily a beer festival; entry likely free, pay per item.

name: National Geographic 'Ultimate Discovery' photo exhibition
venue: One Avenue 卓悦中心, Futian
desc: Touring science-and-nature photography exhibition (150+ works) in a CBD mall; visually engaging and educational for older kids and parents.
notes: Uncertain: only a 2-day overlap (Jul 6-7) and dates conflict across sources. Verify it is still open before relying on it.

name: Banpo Bridge Moonlight Rainbow Fountain 달빛무지개분수
desc: The world's longest bridge fountain: 380 nozzles and 200 LED lights shoot water in time to music over the Han River. A free nightly water-and-light show that delights kids; best after dark.
notes: Daily Mar 16-Oct 31; summer adds extra evening rounds. Weather-dependent.

name: National Museum of Korea: 'Our Dining Table' 우리들의 밥상
venue: National Museum of Korea, Special Exhibition Hall 2
desc: Special exhibition on the origins and evolution of Korean food culture, with ~420 objects including vessels from the Muryeong royal tomb. Food-themed and engaging for children; the grounds have a big park and pond.
notes: Runs Jul 1-Oct 25. A ticket may be required after the Jul 1-5 open house. Note: the Children's Museum is closed Jun 29-Jul 27.

name: 2026 Seoul International Garden Show 서울국제정원박람회
venue: Seoul Forest & Ttukseom Hangang Park, Seongsu
desc: Korea's largest garden festival across Seoul Forest, the Han River park and Seongsu streets. Open green spaces and themed gardens make an easy, free, stroller-friendly family outing.

name: N Seoul Tower Global Night Walk
desc: Evening program at Namsan / N Seoul Tower with night markets, live music and Seoul night views. Combines a gentle cable-car trip with food stalls and entertainment.
notes: Only overlaps Jul 10-11 (ends Jul 11). Observatory entry separately ticketed.

name: Deoksugung Royal Guard Changing Ceremony 왕궁수문장 교대의식
desc: Daily reenactment of the Joseon-era royal guard changing ceremony in full costume with drums and ceremonial weapons at the palace gate. A colorful free spectacle, great for kids.

price.note: gate ceremony free to watch; palace ~1,000 KRW, kids free

notes: Year-round; suspended Mondays and in extreme heat/rain.

name: Musical 'Billy Elliot' 빌리 엘리어트
venue: Blue Square Shinhan Card Hall, Hannam
desc: Stage musical about a working-class boy who discovers ballet, a classic family show with child performers. Performed in Korean.
notes: Runs Apr 12-Jul 26. Korean-language; ticketed ~60,000-150,000 KRW. Check Interpark for exact showtimes.

name: The Roy Kim Show (concert)
desc: Concert by singer-songwriter Roy Kim, known for warm, melodic acoustic pop. A relaxed, all-ages show suitable for families with older children or teens.
notes: Ticketed via Interpark/Yes24. Overlaps Jul 11-12 only.

name: Sungnyemun Gatekeeper Ceremony 숭례문 파수의식
desc: Traditional guard-duty reenactment at Seoul's historic Sungnyemun gate, with costumed guards, drumming and ceremonial routines. A free, central, kid-friendly bit of living history by Namdaemun Market.
